title: Scale of operations
summary: 9609 A Level — economies and diseconomies of scale, optimal scale, and evaluation.
learningObjectives: Evaluate optimal scale for case businesses.

simpleExplanation.title: Bigger isn't always better
simpleExplanation.summary: As output rises, unit costs may fall through technical, financial, and marketing economies — but beyond a point, diseconomies like poor communication and demotivation can raise costs again.
steps: Technical economies: bulk buying, specialisation, indivisibilities.
Financial economies: better loan terms for large firms.
Marketing economies: spread advertising over more output.
Diseconomies: coordination delays, bureaucracy, low morale.

flashcards.back: Unit cost falls as output increases — internal vs external.
Lower interest rates — banks trust large firms more.
National ad campaign cost spread over millions of units.
Slow decisions, duplicated roles, alienated workforce.
Smallest output where unit cost is minimised.
Flow production achieves scale; job production does not.
Cost leadership needs scale; niche strategy may avoid diseconomies.

sections.content: **Scale of operations** (9.1.2) — draw **U-shaped LRAC** sketch if helpful. Ask: has the case firm **passed minimum efficient scale** or hit **diseconomies**?

sections.question: National retailer merges with rival, doubling stores to 1,200. Unit costs fall 8% year one but rise 3% year three. Explain using economies and diseconomies.
sections.solution:
**Year 1 economies:** **Bulk purchasing** from suppliers (technical), **single IT/ad campaign** (marketing/financial), **central warehousing**.

**Year 3 diseconomies:** **Integration chaos** — duplicate HQs, **conflicting cultures** (7.2.4), **slow decisions** (7.1.2 tall hierarchy), **demotivated staff** fearing redundancy (2.2).

**Evaluation:** Merger **theoretically sound** for scale but **management** failed to capture synergies — need **delayering** and **culture programme** before further expansion.

sections.label: Mark a scale of operations question
